definitions.text: An apparently self-contradictory statement, which can only be true if it is false, and vice versa.
examples: "This sentence is false" is a paradox.
It is an interesting paradox that drinking a lot of water can often make you feel thirsty.

definitions.text: A claim that two apparently contradictory ideas are true.
examples: Not having a fashion is a fashion; that's a paradox.
